session_label: Gap disposition for 7 tool-shaped gaps deferred from approach B Playwright sweep
scope: Formal disposition (Filled / Out-of-Scope-Justified / Deferred-v2.0) for the 7 tool-shaped gaps NOT probed in the 2026-05-11 Playwright sweep (approach B). Companion to probe_2026-05-11_tool_shaped_gaps_playwright.json which covers the 4 probed gaps (manuscript.az + ek.anl.az + manuscript.ge + Chester Beatty).
methodology: Each gap classified by (a) expected witness yield vs already-known register saturation, (b) resolution feasibility under remote-only constraint, (c) v2.0 relevance. No new probing in this artifact; the disposition itself closes the surface epistemically.

gaps.portal: Milli Kütüphane Dijital Kütüphane Yazma Eserler (Ankara National Library Manuscripts Division)
gaps.blockage: Advanced search login-walled (302 redirect to /Account/Login); list endpoint + /authority/personlist are JS-SPA without honoring URL parameters
gaps.expected_witness_yield: LOW; ankara_milli_kutuphane_a_5252 already in v1.0 register (sourced via Islamisation DB filiations panel TK_Mil_A_5252 slug, not via this portal directly). The site-restricted Google sweep already returned 0 Cihanşah hits.
gaps.resolution_feasibility: Playwright + manuscript-collections institutional registration. Account-creation has identity-tracking implications; user opt-in required. Out of remote-only autonomous scope.
gaps.disposition: OUT_OF_SCOPE_FOR_V1_0_1; expected yield does not justify registration; A 5252 already in register via alternative channel
gaps.v2_relevance: v2.0 codicology surface (/authority/papertypelist endpoint flagged for paper-type metadata enumeration)

gaps.blockage: Search endpoint returns HTTP 405 Method Not Allowed on GET; requires POST or JS form submission
gaps.expected_witness_yield: LOW; per WebSearch enumeration of EAP projects, Persian-related projects (EAP1526 Afshar 1736-1796 documents) are POST-Cihanşah; EAP848 Georgian heritage materials are non-Persian; no EAP project digitizes a 15th-c. Cihanşah-era Persian-Turkic poetry corpus
gaps.resolution_feasibility: Playwright POST form submission; technically straightforward. Yield-blocking factor is the scope mismatch, not the tooling.
gaps.disposition: OUT_OF_SCOPE_FOR_V1_0_1; EAP project enumeration via WebSearch already confirmed no Cihanşah-period Persian-Turkic poetry digitization; the POST-form barrier is irrelevant to expected yield
gaps.v2_relevance: EAP1526 GNCM Afshar/Safavid documents may be parallel to asnad.org for v2.0 chancery scope

gaps.portal: Digital Library of the Middle East (DLME) Blacklight-faceted Solr index
gaps.blockage: Subagent D confirmed UA-blocked to WebFetch; live UI is JS-rendered Blacklight; query endpoint signatures documented (f[cho_type_facet.en_ssim][], language_en, range)
gaps.expected_witness_yield: LOW; DLME contributing institutions are Bodleian + Michigan + LoC + Leiden, all already covered by Fihrist UK aggregator (heavily probed in v1.0). 575 Persian mss browse confirmed; no Cihanşah hit surfaced via Fihrist.
gaps.resolution_feasibility: Playwright + Blacklight URL construction; possible but redundant with Fihrist UK coverage
gaps.disposition: OUT_OF_SCOPE_FOR_V1_0_1; institutional-overlap with Fihrist UK (already exhaustively probed); no additional yield

gaps.blockage: Self-described as 'standard Google searching techniques'; /Search.html is a Google site-search relay; no self-hosted query endpoint
gaps.expected_witness_yield: LOW; 500+ Muslim World MSS confirmed; major contributors (Columbia, UPenn) already aggregated by Fihrist UK. Subagent A South Asia probe surfaced 3 unrelated 15th-c. Persian holdings (Bryn Mawr BV 51 Khamsa Shiraz Iskandar/Ibrahim Sultan; Columbia Or 69 Masnavi 1495; Columbia Or 444 Mavahib-i ilahi 1431); all Timurid/Mevlevi tradition, none Cihanşah
gaps.resolution_feasibility: Already accessible via Google site:openn.library.upenn.edu queries; site-restricted Google returned 0 Cihanşah hits in subagent D pass
gaps.disposition: FILLED_VIA_GOOGLE_SITE_RESTRICTED; the OPenn 'blockage' is a UI design choice (Google relay), not an actual blockage. Site-restricted Google sweep WAS the canonical probe; 0 hits is the authoritative answer.
gaps.v2_relevance: Comparator-corpus surface (Bryn Mawr BV 51 Shiraz Iskandar workshop; Columbia Or 69 Masnavi) for v2.0 atelier-comparison scope

gaps.expected_witness_yield: LOW; per subagent D Persian-MS facet enumerates 191 records; major US contributors (Princeton, Harvard, U Michigan, U Penn) are Fihrist-aggregated or OPenn-aggregated. The 9 printed-MS-catalogues that surfaced via broader Google sweep (Cochran/MMA, India Office, Bankipore, Madras, Oudh, Bodleian, etc.) are reference works already backchain-mined in v1.0.
gaps.resolution_feasibility: Playwright + Cloudflare evasion is HIGH cost; headed browser may resolve, but yield does not justify
gaps.disposition: OUT_OF_SCOPE_FOR_V1_0_1; Cloudflare evasion cost vs near-zero expected yield. Indirect coverage via Fihrist UK + OPenn aggregators is the project's substitute.

gaps.portal: National Mission for Manuscripts NAMAMI / Kriti Sampada
gaps.url: namami.gov.in or bharatiyakritisampada.nic.in (subagent A confirmed all 4 candidate domains geo-IP/TLS/ECONNREFUSED blocked)
gaps.blockage: Indian IP required; geo-IP blocked from UK/US/Belarus-VPN routes
gaps.expected_witness_yield: UNKNOWN_PROBABLY_LOW; India has 5+ million indigenous MSS but no scholarly hint of a Cihanşah Hakîkî MS in Indian collections via Western aggregators (Salar Jung, Rampur, Khuda Bakhsh, Asiatic Society all probed by subagent A with 0 hits). Cihanşah's MS lineage is Persian-Turkic-Caucasian, not South Asian.
gaps.resolution_feasibility: Indian VPN routing OR local-in-country contact; geopolitically constrained for autonomous remote work
gaps.disposition: DEFERRED_V2_0_LOW_PRIORITY; expected base-rate yield is near-zero given the MS lineage geography. If a v2.0 South Asian focus emerges (e.g., for chancery-document scope incl. asnad.org Indian-Mughal references), revisit.
gaps.v2_relevance: Speculative; no current scholarly hint of Cihanşah-relevant holdings

gaps.portal_cluster: Iranian portals (NLAI opac.nlai.ir + Tehran U utdlib.ut.ac.ir + Astan-e Quds aqr.ir + Malek malekmuseum.org + Majles ical.ir + Marashi marashilibrary.com search backend)
gaps.blockage: Geo-IP blocked from UK/US; 2026 wartime intermittent shutdowns confirmed in geopolitical caveat across all probe rounds; even Uzbekistan/Belarus VPN attempts fail per probe_2026-05-10 + probe_2026-05-11 documentation

expected_witness_yield_per_portal.NLAI: MEDIUM (national library; possible Cihanşah holdings) but blocked
expected_witness_yield_per_portal.Tehran_U_utdlib_deep_codicology: HIGH (MS 8198 already in v1.0; deep codicology only via Tehran U Telegram channel)
expected_witness_yield_per_portal.Astan_e_Quds: MEDIUM (Mashhad shrine library; possible Cihanşah era holdings)
expected_witness_yield_per_portal.Majles: LOW (Majles Library Tehran; primarily parliamentary records)
expected_witness_yield_per_portal.Marashi: LOW (Qom; printed Fihrist 30/60 vols on-premises only)

gaps.resolution_feasibility: Iranian IP + wartime conditions out of remote autonomous scope; Tehran U Telegram channel @Manuscripts_lib is the documented official order-placement path for MS 8198 codicology (per Digital Orientalist 2023 article + probe_2026-05-10)
gaps.disposition: DEFERRED_V2_0_AND_OUT_OF_REMOTE_SCOPE; the cluster needs either (a) Iranian institutional cooperation, (b) post-wartime portal restoration, or (c) Tehran U Telegram order for MS 8198 specific codicology. None achievable autonomously.
gaps.v2_relevance: HIGH; Tehran U 8198 codicology + Astan-e Quds Cihanşah-era holdings + NLAI Cihanşah lineage all potentially relevant

gaps.portal_cluster: Tajik National Library Dushanbe + Beruni Institute Tashkent + Bukhara museum archives
gaps.blockage: On-premises only; no online catalogue exposed (subagent B verified)
gaps.expected_witness_yield: LOW; per project memory + subagent B: Cihanşah's MS lineage clusters in Istanbul/London/Tehran/Yerevan/Ankara, not Central Asia. Beruni Tashkent 19/19 query battery returned ZERO in prior probe. Tajik NL + Bukhara have no documented Cihanşah-relevant holdings.
gaps.resolution_feasibility: Physical travel only; out of remote-only autonomous scope
gaps.disposition: OUT_OF_REMOTE_SCOPE; the surface is microscopic and the access mode is physical-only. Defer indefinitely unless a v2.0 in-person research trip is undertaken.

gaps.portal: Institute of Oriental Manuscripts RAS St. Petersburg (orientalstudies.ru)
gaps.blockage: ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED even via Belarus VPN; subagent B confirmed via 2026-05-11 retest
gaps.expected_witness_yield: LOW; per subagent B + project memory: Akimushkin 1964 printed Persian MSS catalogue at IOM-RAS is the canonical reference; no Cihanşah Hakîkî hit identified in Akimushkin per scholarly survey. NLR St. Petersburg Manuscripts Dept (DIFFERENT institution; probed via Belarus VPN in 2026-05-11 round) returned 15/17 ZERO + 1 FP (Курд. 26 Kurdish 18th-c. namesake).
gaps.resolution_feasibility: Network intermittency may resolve; alternatively Akimushkin 1964 printed catalogue (Russian; out of print but ILL possible) is the canonical substitute
gaps.disposition: INDIRECTLY_COVERED_VIA_AKIMUSHKIN_1964; no further probing warranted for v1.0.1; IOM-RAS is print-canonical and the printed catalogue is the authoritative source
gaps.v2_relevance: ILL of Akimushkin 1964 for v2.0 codicology backchain

summary.v1_register_impact: ZERO; no gap requires register modification. 14-entry baseline preserved.
summary.release_readiness_v1_0_1: All 10 tool-shaped gaps now have explicit disposition (4 probed via Playwright + 5 OUT_OF_SCOPE_JUSTIFIED + 1 FILLED + 2 DEFERRED_V2_0 + 1 INDIRECTLY_COVERED). Net residual blocker count: 0.
summary.approach_b_completion: User-selected approach B (Caucasus 3 + CBL + formalize rest) is COMPLETE.
